name: ooh.directory
note: A hand-kept directory of personal blogs on every subject imaginable. Open it when you want to fall down a rabbit hole of someone's twenty-year-old website about lighthouses or lichen.

name: Marginalia Search
note: A search engine that deliberately favours the small, text-heavy, non-commercial web. Type in something you love and you'll surface pages no algorithm would ever show you.

name: Wiby
note: A search engine for the old, handmade web — pages built by one person for the love of it. Hit the surprise button and let it take you somewhere strange.

name: Gossip's Web
note: A cosy directory of indie and personal sites, sorted by feel as much as topic. A good place to remember the internet is still made by people.

name: Are.na
note: A quiet, ad-free space where people assemble visual collections of ideas. Wander other people's channels and watch unexpected connections form.

name: Cool Tools
note: Decades of recommendations for unusual, genuinely useful things — tools, books, techniques. You'll leave with at least one thing you didn't know you wanted.

name: Bartosz Ciechanowski
note: Explorable explanations of how things actually work — clocks, light, GPS — each one an interactive essay you can play with. Set aside an afternoon.

name: Gwern
note: One person's sprawling, deeply linked research notebook on everything from statistics to cats. Follow a thread and see how far down it goes.

name: Maggie Appleton
note: A digital garden of illustrated essays on ideas, tools for thought, and how we think online. Wander the patches and let one essay lead to the next.

name: The Public Domain Review
note: Collections of curious, beautiful things long out of copyright — strange maps, forgotten illustrations, odd old books. Pure unhurried browsing.
